channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Snowbirds to Perform Final Show in Moose Jaw Before Retiring Tutor Jets
item.description: The Royal Canadian Air Force's Snowbirds are set to perform their final hometown show in Moose Jaw, Saskatchewan, on Saturday as their CT-114 Tutor jets prepare for retirement. Federal Defence Ministe...

item.title: Young Woman's Death at Minister's Residence Under Investigation
item.description: A young woman named Mary Habbila died under mysterious circumstances inside the residence of Nigeria's Works Minister, Senator David Umahi, in Ebonyi State. The incident occurred on June 27, 2026, a d...

item.title: Canberra Raiders adjust backline for pivotal NRL match against Canterbury Bulldogs
item.description: The Canberra Raiders have made a late tactical adjustment to their backline ahead of a crucial NRL clash against the Canterbury Bulldogs. The reshuffle comes as the Raiders seek to maintain their seas...

item.title: Yanga appoints experienced South African coach Manqoba Mngqithi
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga) have appointed Manqoba Mngqithi as their new head coach ahead of the 2026/27 season. The 55-year-old, who previously coached Golden Arrows and Mamelodi Sundowns, brings over a d...

item.title: Cape Town boosts youth opportunities with R227 million library upgrades and BPO sector support
item.description: The City of Cape Town has announced a R227 million investment to modernise its 102 public libraries, which attract over seven million annual visitors. This initiative includes expanding the Hector Pet...

item.title: Security Concerns Over Trump's New Qatar-Gifted Air Force One
item.description: US President Donald Trump faces mounting security concerns regarding his newly gifted Qatar aircraft, which lacks the advanced countermeasures of his older jet. The retrofitted Boeing 747-8, gifted by...

item.title: Mimiyuuuh Reflects on the Meaningful Gift She Gave Her Parents
item.description: Mimiyuuuh, a prominent Filipino celebrity, recently shared insights about the most special gift she gave her parents, highlighting its emotional significance. The article, published on July 11, 2026, ...

item.title: A Novel Exploring Resilience Amid Bengal's Partition
item.description: Alka Saraogi's Hindi novel 'Register Me as Kulbhushan', translated by John Vater, delves into the complexities of identity and human resilience during India's Partition and subsequent political upheav...

item.title: England face Norway in World Cup quarter-final at Miami's Hard Rock Stadium
item.description: England will take on Norway in the World Cup 2026 quarter-finals at Miami’s Hard Rock Stadium on Saturday, 11 July, kicking off at 10pm BST. The match follows England’s dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexic...

item.title: Typhoon Inday and Habagat Cause 17 Deaths, 9 Missing in Philippines
item.description: A powerful typhoon system comprising Tropical Storm Inday and the Southwest Monsoon (Habagat) has caused significant casualties and displacement in the Philippines. According to official reports, at l...

item.title: Philippine fishermen face ongoing challenges at Scarborough Shoal despite 2016 tribunal ruling
item.description: A decade after the 2016 international tribunal ruling that invalidated China's maritime claims in the South China Sea, Filipino fishermen continue to face harassment and restrictions at Scarborough Sh...

item.title: Brisbane father finds purpose after wife's cancer death by supporting charity
item.description: Joel Hockey, a 48-year-old Brisbane father-of-four, has found unexpected meaning in his grief after losing his wife Natalie to bowel cancer. Weeks after her death, Hockey discovered a stack of books l...

item.title: South African coach Manqoba Mngqithi joins Tanzanian league as Young Africans head coach
item.description: Manqoba Mngqithi, the former Golden Arrows coach who guided the KwaZulu-Natal side to a top-eight finish in the Betway Premiership, has been appointed as the new head coach of Tanzanian Premier League...

item.title: NYOTA Fund Discrepancy: Deputy President Kindiki Attributes Shortfall to Technical Error
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki was caught off guard during a NYOTA disbursement event in Nakuru when a beneficiary revealed she received KSh 19,000 instead of the promised KSh 22,000. The discrepanc...

item.title: San Jose Driver Sentenced to Jail for Insurance Fraud and Road Rage Brake-Check Crash
item.description: A San Jose man was sentenced to 60 days in county jail after prosecutors said he intentionally caused a crash with a semi-truck during a road rage incident and then attempted to defraud his insurance ...

item.title: Japan advances reusable rocket technology with successful test flight
item.description: Japan's space agency, JAXA, has successfully conducted its first test flight for an experimental reusable rocket, marking a significant step in reducing space launch costs. The RV-X rocket, developed ...

item.title: Kisii Medical Student Dies Suddenly During Clinical Placement; Classmates Remember Her Compassion
item.description: Mercy Chebet, a student pursuing a Certificate in Healthcare Services Support (Nurse Aide) at Nairobi College of Medical Studies – Kisii Campus, passed away following a short illness. The incident occ...

item.title: Adding Personality to Modern New Build Apartments
item.description: As construction costs rise, soulless white-cube housing is becoming the norm in cities, prompting new-build residents to personalise their spaces. The article explores how individuals are transforming...

item.title: Justin Baldoni defends his ex-co-star amid controversy over Blake Lively's allegations
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing public discourse surrounding Justin Baldoni, an actor and director, who recently broke a two-year silence to publicly criticize Blake Lively following her allegations...

item.title: North Bay Hosts Free FIFA World Cup Fan Fest for Quarterfinals
item.description: North Bay, a city in northern Ontario, is set to become the host of a free FIFA World Cup Fan Fest in July 2026. The event will take place ahead of the quarterfinal matches, offering fans an opportuni...
